input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` takes `Light` and `Dark` values and automatically switches when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

The equivalent in code-behind or C# markup:

```csharp
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

var button = new Button { Text = "Get Started" };
button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty, Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"), Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
button.SetAppTheme(Button.TextColorProperty, Colors.White, Colors.Black);
```

**Signature:** `SetAppTheme(BindableProperty, lightValue, darkValue)`

## Scaling with resource dictionaries

For larger apps, define theme colors in `App.xaml` so you don't repeat hex values:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>
    <!-- Won't work — AppThemeBinding isn't valid inline in a Color resource -->
</Application.Resources>
```

Instead, use **merged dictionaries with `AppThemeBinding`** at the style level:

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <Style TargetType="Label">
        <Setter Property="TextColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />
    </Style>
    <Style TargetType="ContentPage">
        <Setter Property="BackgroundColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}" />
    </Style>
</Application.Resources>
```

This applies theming globally without touching individual pages.

## Detecting the current theme in C#

```csharp
AppTheme currentTheme = Application.Current.RequestedTheme;
// Returns AppTheme.Light, AppTheme.Dark, or AppTheme.Unspecified

// React to runtime changes:
Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged += (s, e) =>
{
    // e.RequestedTheme holds the new value
};
```

**Key takeaway:** Use `AppThemeBinding` in XAML (or `SetAppTheme` in C#) on each property that needs to vary. The framework handles the switching automatically — no manual event handling required.
